summary: Backend testing for BPOM Jember Inventory: 26/26 tests passed covering auth, seed, items CRUD + duplicate kode, stock card, incoming goods (stok increment + MASUK movements + total_nilai), public SPB submission with nomor format NNNN/PSD/<roman>/<year>, SPB approval creating SBBK with proper nomor + reducing stok + KELUAR movements, insufficient-stock rejection (400), SPB REJECT flow, assets CRUD (public GET allowed), kondisi inspection + invalid kondisi 400, QR PNG generation, dashboard stats shape (all required keys), reports, users RBAC (admin-only list, role patch, delete-self blocked), upload + serve via Emergent Object Storage.

minor.issue: Returns HTTP 400 (correct) but no uniqueness index in DB; uniqueness only enforced at app layer (db.items.create_index('kode', unique=False)).
If item_id is invalid/non-existent, request still succeeds: $inc creates no doc but movement is logged. Consider validating item_id exists.
total_nilai uses item.harga (purchase price stored on Item, not weighted-avg purchase price from incoming). Acceptable but worth documenting.
session cookie set with SameSite=None,Secure=true and HttpOnly; works for browser, but no CSRF token for state-changing endpoints (login uses session-id from emergent only).

action_items: Optional: add Mongo unique index on items.kode (currently unique=False) to prevent races.
Optional: validate item_id existence inside POST /api/incoming lines before $inc/movement logging.
Optional: index db.spb on status and db.movements on created_at for performance at scale.
Consider: PATCH /api/items uses full ItemIn (requires kode/nama). Could relax to partial update for UX.
Consider: /api/admin/seed is open to any authenticated user when items count is 0; gate behind admin role explicitly.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 632 lines and houses every domain (auth, items, SPB, assets, dashboard, reports, upload, seed). Should be split into modular routers (auth.py, items.py, spb.py, assets.py, dashboard.py).
require_role('admin_gudang','admin') still requires explicit 'admin' arg because dep also auto-allows admin via fallback inside; the explicit duplication is harmless but redundant.
Mongo _id is consistently excluded via projections {'_id': 0} and clean() helper - good.
All endpoints under /api prefix - correct ingress routing.
All env vars loaded with os.environ[...] (fail-fast) and frontend uses REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL - good.
SPB nomor sequence uses count_documents which can race under concurrent submissions; consider an atomic counter doc using $inc with findOneAndUpdate upsert.
Approval path doesn't roll back stok if a later step fails (no transaction). Risk: partial reductions on error. Use a Mongo transaction for atomicity.
Asset photo upload returns server-relative '/api/files/<path>' but no auth required to serve files (potential info leak if storage paths are guessable). Consider auth-gating /api/files/{path}.
test_credentials: Test admin session created via mongosh: token=test_session_1781752022836 (also accepts the orchestrator-provided test_session_1781751917429). Reads from env TEST_ADMIN_TOKEN.
seed_data_creation: POST /api/admin/seed used; already-populated state handled (test accepts 200 or 400). 8 items + 5 assets exist.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully working at https://warehouse-hub-256.preview.emergentagent.com/api. Reuse /app/backend/tests/test_inventory_api.py + conftest.py. Pass TEST_ADMIN_TOKEN env var with a fresh mongosh-created session, or create one with the snippet in /app/auth_testing.md. Tests create their own SPBs and items prefixed with TEST_ - safe to re-run. Frontend not tested by request.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. All required backend flows function as specified: auth, seed, items CRUD with duplicate-kode 400, stock card, incoming (stok + movements + total_nilai), public SPB POST with NNNN/PSD/<roman>/<year>, approval creating SBBK + reducing stok + KELUAR movements + 400 on insufficient stock, REJECT path, assets CRUD with public GET, kondisi update + invalid kondisi 400, QR PNG, dashboard stats with all keys, reports, users RBAC, upload + serve.
